reasoning: FrontierMath was launched in Nov 2024 and evaluated state-of-the-art models (like Opus, GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5 Pro, and implicitly early test-time models) yielding <2% success. The benchmark comprises deeply rigorous problems requiring hours or days of expert mathematician effort. Getting from <2% to >80% in roughly one year (by end of 2025) would require an unbelievable capability jump—effectively automating complex mathematical research—which is beyond the expected progress rate of one or two normal model generations. Even extremely fast-moving capabilities benchmarks, such as SWE-bench, have required intense scaffolding to reach ~40-50% in roughly a year from a <2% starting point, while 80% represents near-complete mastery. To hit 80% on expert math, one would need systems exhibiting AGI-level long-horizon coherence without reasoning collapse, making this very unlikely by Dec 2025.
